content.problemStatement:
Chef has a favourite positive integer XXX.

He wondered if there are two positive integers aaa, bbb such that:


1≤a,b≤8⋅10181\le a, b \le 8 \cdot 10^{18}1≤a,b≤8⋅1018;
The values aaa &\&& bbb and a⊕ba \oplus ba⊕b are **positive** and **divisible** by XXX. Note that &\&& and ⊕\oplus⊕ denote bitwise and and bitwise xor operations respectively.
You need to help Chef find these two numbers.

If there are multiple solutions, you can output any of them. Under the given constraints, it can be proven that at least one solution always exists.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer XXX — Chef's favourite number.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, two space-separated integers aaa and bbb satisfying the given constraint.


If there are multiple solutions, you can output any of them.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1 ≤ X ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output two space‑separated positive integers a and b on a single line such that 1 ≤ a, b ≤ 8·10^18, a & b > 0, a ⊕ b > 0 and both a & b and a ⊕ b are divisible by X. Any valid pair is accepted.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For X=2 we output a=2, b=6. a&b = 2, a⊕b = 4, both divisible by 2. For X=5 we output a=5, b=15. a&b = 5, a⊕b = 10, both divisible by 5.

solution.approach:
For a given X we can choose a = X and b = 3·X. Because X and 2·X have no common set bits, 3·X = X | 2·X. Hence:
- a & b = X & (X | 2·X) = X, which is positive and divisible by X.
- a ⊕ b = X ⊕ (X | 2·X) = 2·X, also positive and divisible by X.
Both a and b are ≤ 3·10^9 < 8·10^18, satisfying the limits. The construction works for every X, guaranteeing a solution.
The algorithm simply reads X, computes a = X and b = 3*X, and prints them.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
